Distributed by: Amazon MGM Studios, Sony Pictures Releasing International
Comment:
I went to see this movie because so many friends and colleagues recommended it. Even more than a month after its release, on a Tuesday night, the theater was still quite full—clearly a sign of a successful film.
It’s a great story with beautiful visuals. I really loved Dr. Grace, and Rocky completely stole the show. I also couldn’t help but appreciate all of Ryan Gosling’s outfits throughout the film. Overall, it’s a visually stunning and emotionally warm movie that left a strong impression on me.

It was a beautiful and touching story. I didn’t expect the main character, Mike, to die, which made it even more emotional. I cried when Claire was singing at his funeral, and I felt really anxious watching her stand in front of the house where the two accidents had happened. The ending was heartbreaking, and the movie finished right as my flight landed in LA. That timing actually made me feel physically uncomfortable—my stomach even started to hurt. I guess I’ve learned my lesson: no sad movies on flights.

I heard about this movie on the show The Offer and have always wanted to see it. A friend I met today confirmed that it is a great movie. So I watched it tonight. It is a beautiful love story. You can feel love flowing throughout the movie. Great story, great music, great pictures, great acting, and great lines.
It was great. I like the story, the picture, and how Paul and Luke walk together.

This movie makes me nervous, but never in tears. The sad parts were presented with grace and dignity. I like the way they tell the stories. But the story wasn't very intriguing to me. I may have different understandings in the future with more life experience. A funny thing was that I watched The Little Prince earlier that day, and it also features a plane and an aviator in the desert. What a coincidence!

A beautiful, warm, and sad story. It makes the audience reflect. We often commercialize ourselves after we grow up and forget to enjoy all the colors of our lives. The Little Prince is a well-known story. I read the book more than ten years ago and never knew there was a movie until yesterday, when I saw it on P+. So I decided to watch it right away. I am happy that the elderly retired aviator and the little girl's mom support her so well with love, patience, and understanding.

Movie Name: Indiana Jones and the Raiders of the Lost Ark
Comment: I started watching it two weeks ago and finished the first half. Today, I finished the second half. I decided to watch it because I was wondering where the ark is now. A friend said I needed to watch Indiana Jones, lol. I've heard of this name for a long time, and it is a Paramount movie, so why not watch it? It could be a little scary, but it is a warm and fun story. Dr. Jones is handsome, funny, brave, smart, and kind.

I went to the theater to watch Avatar: The Way of Water, but slept for at least two hours. I never understood the story. However, I still decided to watch the new Avatar film because it is successful and one of my friends strongly recommended it. After the first 25 minutes, I wanted to fall asleep again, but I tried my best to stay awake. Then, I got to understand the story and began to enjoy it. The story was fine. But the pictures were very beautiful.

Since I have been studying the story of David every week for several months, I thought I needed to watch this movie. It was for children, so the plots are simple. But the music and the pictures are both satisfying for me. I enjoyed it.

This movie is different. You would expect something might happen, but it won't. I couldn't appreciate until I watched some YouTube videos with detailed explanations of what happended in the movie and how the story was created. It was a great!

I've wanted to see this film for a long time, but never did. I knew it from the show The Offer. One of my colleagues at Paramount said he didn't like this movie because it was too dark. After watching The Godfather and several other dark and horror movies, I am no longer afraid of watching most movies. Chinatown is a great movie. There were some dark elements, and the ending wasn't what we wanted, but that was the reality in many cases. It would be boring if every movie had a happy ending.

This is a great film. A great story and great pictures. The two girls were so beautiful. I was amazed by Isabelle's beauty and learned that she was played by Sophie Marceau, whose name I had heard so many times. I also liked Mel Gibson, who was the producer, the director, and the major actor. I couldn't imagine one person could play three important roles in a film and do such a great job! Amazing!

Movie Name: The Kid Stays in the Picture
Comment: This is the life story of Robert Evans, a former executive of Paramount Pictures. I liked him in the show The Offer.
This one was fine. The story was not as polished as the previous two. Also, we didn't like Michael's daughter, Mary.
This is my favorite one. The stories of two godfathers were excellent. Great story, great acting, and great pictures! This movie speaks to my heart.

This is my second time watching it. The first time was many years ago in China with my family. I watched it very carefully this time and did extensive research to better understand it, including watching more than 20 YouTube videos that explain the movie. It is truly a masterpiece! My favorite scene is Micheal meets Appollonia for the first time.
A classical action spy film.
Beautiful pictures. The style was kinda like a combination of Harry Potter and Barbie.
Too many visual effects. The story was not as good as the older one.

Distributed by: Paramount Pictures and Warner Bros. Pictures
Comment: A masterpiece directed by Christopher Nolan. I loved it.
A great epic film. I love the story, the acting, and the picture. I was probably in tears several times.
I love it. Audrey Hepburn was cute and beautiful. Also, I love seeing the streets of New York City in the old days.

It has an engaging story and breathtaking visuals. Watching it on the big screen was an amazing experience, and I felt so proud that it was Paramount that made it. The song "If I Fall," which played at the end, instantly got stuck in my head, and I couldn’t stop listening to it. I've been playing it on repeat while driving, imagining that every car on the road has a soul.

I watched The Sound of Music because Reagan mentioned it was his favorite movie. The last time I saw it was over ten years ago. It's a classic film, full of fun and with a great love story. I admire how Maria is kind and stays true to herself, bringing energy and passion to everything she does. Her strong faith helps her face the challenges of the mischievous children and the strict, cold demeanor of the captain without fear.

A friend recommended Reagan to me, and it was a great experience. I didn't realize he was an actor before becoming President of the U.S. This movie taught me a lot about the country and its history. I also really enjoyed seeing the relationship between Reagan and Nancy—their love and support for each other is truly inspiring and amazing!

Distributed by: Warner Bros., Warner Bros. Pictures, Mister Smith Entertainment
Comment: This is the story of the great Indian mathematician Srinivasa Ramanujan. Mathematics was his true calling, and he pursued it with unparalleled dedication. His experiences suggest the presence of a higher power and the possiblity of a profound connection with it. It's tragic that Ramanujan's life was cut short so early. His life also reminds us that even those who love us can sometimes unintentionally cause harm, so it's essential to prioritize our own well-being and self-care.

This is a timeless masterpiece. Twelve years ago, I first encountered the story through the movie Les Misérables (2012) and fell in love with it immediately. Today, I watched the musical version, and it was terrific. It touched my heart and soul. My words cannot express my gratitude to all the passionate and dedicated people who made this story and show accessible to me.

Even without extensive knowledge of the Marvel Universe, I found it to be a fun movie. Despite the numerous violent fight scenes, they were neither scary nor sad. After working for a movie company for 2.5 years and visiting three movie studios in Hollywood, I learned to take movie stories less seriously because I know they are fictional. The filmmakers just want us to enjoy ourselves!

Movie Name: South Park: The End of Obesity
Comment: I deeply empathize with those struggling with obesity. It's important to understand that willpower alone often cannot overcome the complex chemical reactions in the body.

Often, people are bound and controlled by their fears. If they can free themselves from these fears, they will discover a new world full of possibilities and attain inner peace. I missed the first 30 minutes of the most famous chariot race, so I need to watch it again.

This is a super fun movie. It reminded me of the great times I had at Super Nintendo World. I should have watched it before visiting Super Nintendo World at Universal Studios. But it's okay. I will visit it again and have more fun next time!
This is a great story. It teaches us to awalys stay kind and have hope. I can't wait to see the second part next week.

This movie is truly inspiring. It shows how when you have a dream and pursue it with passion and diligence, the world supports you. I saw the power of kind words from a teacher and the profound, silent love between a father and his son.
Watching it for the second time with friends on our big new TV! I love the way Robert solves problems.
Simple and funny.
I watched a horror movie, but I wasn't scared—it was surprisingly warm and cute. This was also my first movie experience on the Paramount Lot.
This is a great story. It moved me deeply, bringing tears to my eyes multiple times as I watched. I should write a blog post for it.

Movie Name: The Chronicles of Narnia: The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe
Comment: We learn and grow by facing difficult challenges. It's nice to experience this growth in another world without it costing any time in our reality.
I saw the power of faith and the importance of a good leader.

A great story from the Bible about Moses. I love how he accepts who he is and follows his heart, giving up all the wealth, power, and privileges. After making this decision, his life moves on, and he settles with what he has.
A story of a successful failure. I was amazed by how they managed to stay calm and complete their work under immense stress and uncertainty. This ability is crucial for achieving success in life!

A heartwarming and healing movie for kids and families. Each child grows up with an imaginary friend (IF) born from their dreams and imagination. An IF can take any form and possess any skills the child desires. They can see and accompany each other. However, as children grow up, they often become busy with life and lose connection with their IF. This movie tells the story of an 11-year-old girl who helps IFs reconnect with their grown-up owners by rekindling cherished childhood memories.

Distributed by: Allied Stars, Enigma Productions, Warner Bros., The Ladd Company, Warner Brothers
Comment: This is a great story about passion, faith, and commitment! Abraham was able to win the gold medal in Olympics in the end after trying very hard and the training day and night to achieve his goal and realize his stream. He succeeded and became the winner. However, this is just a story about the winner. What about other runners who didn't win the game? My friend provided a good point. This is just the story about this guy. Other people had their own life stories, and they won in their ways.

The jokes in the movie do not make much sense to me. I decided to watched it because of Seinfeld but was kinda disappointed. It was a relaxing comedy movie. You barely need to use your brain to watch it.

Distributed by: Sony Pictures Classics, Warner Bros. Pictures, United International Pictures, Memento Films
Comment: This is a quiet and romantic movie. My favorite lines are: "We rip out so much of ourselves to be cured of things faster that we go bankrupt by the age of 30 and have less to offer each time we start with someone new. But to make yourself feel nothing so as not to feel anything. What a waste!"
I love this movie, the story, and the music. It's a simple and warm story about self-growth and passion. The background in New York City reminds me of the good times I spent there last summer.

Movie Name: In the Land of Saints and Sinners
Comment: Calm, Crime
Powerful story, impressive pictures, and superb acting. Watching on IMAX it for the second time.
